witnesses.description: A finite prefix of a_n = n/(n+1), bounded above by 1.
A finite tail of a_n = n/(n+1) whose gap to 1 is below 1/4.

checks.claim: The listed finite prefix is strictly increasing and bounded above by 1.
checks.notes: The validator checks a_n = n/(n+1), adjacent strict inequalities, and every upper-bound inequality.

checks.claim: The displayed finite prefix supremum is 6/7 at index 6.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes the maximum over the listed finite prefix.

checks.claim: For n = 4..8, the listed values are within 1/4 of the limit 1.
checks.notes: The validator checks a_n = n/(n+1) and recomputes the largest listed tail gap as 1/5.

checks.claim: The finite prefix is bounded above by 5/6.

data.certificate: The resource regression parses the source SMT-LIB artifact, emits UnsatFarkas evidence, and independently checks the certificate.

checks.notes: The validator replays the finite prefix to compute a_6 = 6/7; the source artifact checks that 6/7 <= 5/6 is impossible through QF_LRA/Farkas evidence.

checks.claim: The malformed finite tail starting at n = 2 is within epsilon = 1/4 of the proposed limit 1.

data.certificate: The resource regression parses the source SMT-LIB artifact, emits UnsatFarkas evidence, and independently checks the certificate.

checks.notes: The validator replays a_2 = 2/3 and computes tail gap 1/3, which exceeds epsilon 1/4 by 1/12; the source artifact checks the nonpositive-excess contradiction through QF_LRA/Farkas evidence.

checks.claim: The general monotone convergence theorem for bounded monotone real sequences requires future Lean resources.

data.target_theorem_shape: every bounded monotone sequence of real numbers converges to its supremum
data.future_checker: Lean module with no sorryAx dependencies

checks.notes: The finite rows check only listed prefixes and finite tail gaps; completeness and quantified convergence remain proof-assistant territory.
